shape_decode: cmp=W, vp=L, vpc=W, naive=_ : cmplog and value_profile_cmplog resolve, value_profile blocks, naive non-decisive => i2s-pro literal family (the only technique delta between the winners {cmplog,vpc} and the loser {value_profile} is I2SRandReplace). Label-source = resolve pattern; G2 verification = cmplog-vs-naive corpus byte footprint, independent of the vpc/vp pattern.

round0_outcome_reviewed.note: Round-0 was NOT all-inconclusive: operand_enrichment DID discriminate 3 of 7 (all three are FIXED-HEAD-OFFSET distinctive multibyte literals: 'scheme/HTTP' at 0x06-0x2b, 'scheme://authority' at 0x06-0x1b, 'pHYs' FOURCC at 0x25-0x28). The remaining 4 each have a gate the tool's target/decoy-byte model genuinely cannot represent (variable/deep operand position, inequality with no target byte, or a corpus-ambient literal). The libpng/libxml2 branches were scored ON SERVER B (their corpora are on-disk there), so this is NOT an off-server gap.

hypotheses.label: I2SRandReplace splices the exact logged comparand literal into a fixed head gate offset; VP's CMP_MAP Hamming gradient cannot reach the exact value within budget => cmplog corpus enriched in the target byte vs naive

params.scope: per W/L gate offset, log2(target_frac_cmplog / target_frac_naive) over the cmplog-vs-naive corpus; sign-preserving max-magnitude aggregation isolates the single most-differentiating (I2S operand) offset.
params.note: Rule and threshold UNCHANGED from round-0 — they correctly discriminated 3 of 7 and sit inside the registry's validated separation band (pro median +3.12 / anti -0.55, 88% separation). Lowering the cutoff to capture the 4 failures would break that band and chase labels (G3); we do NOT touch it.

hypotheses.prediction: i2s-pro => signed_target_enrich >= 1.0 : the cmplog corpus carries the exact target literal byte at the fixed gate offset at a higher rate than naive, because I2SRandReplace plants the logged comparand there and VP/havoc cannot. A 'VP-gradient-was-sufficient' or 'literal-is-corpus-ambient' alternative predicts signed_target_enrich ~ 0 (no cmplog-vs-naive delta). The sign+magnitude discriminates. G2: this is a cmplog-vs-naive corpus byte-frequency comparison, fully independent of the vpc/value_profile resolve pattern that defined the shape.

non_scorable.reason: The I2S operand is a DUPLICATE 'IHDR' chunk-type appearing a SECOND time at a layout-dependent (variable) downstream offset, not a fixed head offset. operand_enrichment auto-derives its gate signature from W/L head-byte consensus (head=48); for a variable-position second-IHDR the consensus locks onto the incidental shared PNG-header prefix (the FIRST IHDR / signature bytes, identical in W and L) rather than the gate operand, so the scored offsets carry no I2S delta (target==decoy across arms => enrichment ~0). The mechanism is real but its footprint is a VARIABLE-OFFSET structural repeat, which no available descriptor (byte_freq_ratio is fixed-offset; value_distance_reached needs a single fixed operand window) can represent. decidable:false by tool reach, NOT by mechanism doubt or off-server corpus.

Inequality gate `length != 32`: the WINNING value is 'any 4-byte value != 32', so there is NO single target literal byte for operand_enrichment's target/decoy model. The tool found a fixed-offset signature on the shared cHRM FOURCC (present in both W and L => target==decoy, enrichment ~0) and scored it as rule_not_met, but that is the wrong gate. The actual length field is a variable big-endian integer with no consensus byte; value_distance_reached's numeric (magnitude-gap) mode is framed for a vp/vpc gradient WINNER, whereas here vp is the BLOCKER and the win is an I2S step-substitution, so its winner_closer framing does not fit. decidable:false by tool reach.

Single-byte equality `name[0] == ':'` (0x3a), but name[0] sits at a VARIABLE, DTD-scaffold-dependent position deep in the input (after <?xml ...><!DOCTYPE ...>), not at a fixed head offset. The W and L seeds share the identical DTD scaffold prefix, so the head-byte consensus locks onto scaffold bytes (target==decoy) and misses the deep, variable colon position. The operand is width-1, which is chance-alignment prone even if a window tool were applied. No available descriptor can pin a single-byte operand at a variable deep offset. decidable:false by tool reach.

GENUINE low-enrichment, not a tool defect. The gate is a fixed-head contiguous 4-byte 'pop3' scheme literal at 0x06-0x09 — exactly operand_enrichment's sweet spot — yet signed_target_enrich fell below 1.0. The honest read: a scheme prefix is CORPUS-AMBIENT in curl (virtually every reaching seed in naive's corpus already carries some scheme at the head, including 'pop3'-family bytes), so naive_target_frac is high and the cmplog-vs-naive log2 ratio washes out. This is the alternative the prediction is built to catch ('literal-is-corpus-ambient => enrich ~0'), so the negative is a valid discriminating outcome, NOT evidence to lower the threshold. curl_505 stays inconclusive/rule_not_met under the unchanged rule (G3: an honest negative is the correct outcome). Sibling curl_297/468 enriched strongly precisely because their distinctive port/HTTP/authority literals are NOT corpus-ambient — the contrast confirms the rule is doing real work.

notes.merge: Single hypothesis (collapsed the 3 prior Pass-B clusters: i2s_fourcc_chunk_dispatch, i2s_exact_scalar_operand_substitution, i2s_url_structure_assembly). They split on byte_signature (contiguous FOURCC vs single-byte/derived scalar vs dispersed URL scaffold), but operand_enrichment's per_gate_offset metric scores all three identically when the operand is a FIXED-HEAD-OFFSET distinctive literal — and that is exactly the dividing line between the 3 it validated and the 4 it could not. No real-data measurement discriminates the byte_signature subtypes among the validated members, so they remain one hypothesis.
notes.round2_rationale: Task framing implied all 4 inconclusive came from an operand-enrichment failure on an i2s-pro shape where it 'should have shown'. Re-reading the actual assignments_s4/sB shows 3 of 7 DID validate strongly (2.587 / 3.284 / 14.664). The 4 failures are NOT a mis-derived offset or wrong threshold on the validated mechanism; they are 4 genuinely different gate forms the tool's fixed-offset target-byte model cannot represent: (1) variable-offset structural repeat (libpng_3949 duplicate IHDR), (2) inequality with no target byte (libpng_3970 length!=32), (3) variable deep single-byte operand (libxml2_6910 deep DTD colon), (4) a fixed-offset but corpus-ambient literal (curl_505 'pop3'). Three are decidable:false by tool reach; one (curl_505) is an honest discriminating negative.
notes.fix_a_considered: Re-deriving the gate offset from signature.operand_literal instead of head-consensus was considered (fix-a). It does not help: 3949/6910 operands are at VARIABLE positions (no fixed offset to pin), 3970 has no target byte at all, and 505 already pins the correct offset and genuinely under-enriches. So an offset-pinned variant cannot rescue any of the 4.
notes.fix_b_considered: value_distance_reached (Hamming/magnitude closure) was considered for curl_505 and libpng_3970. It is framed for a vp/vpc GRADIENT winner (winner_closer expects vp closest); here vp is the BLOCKER and the win is an I2S step-substitution, so the closure framing inverts and does not cleanly discriminate I2S-pro from VP-sufficiency. struct_size_and_token_count (joint_necessity) is for vpc-needs-both assembly depth, which this shape is not (cmplog alone resolves => not joint). Neither yields a discriminable rule for these 4, so no second hypothesis is added.
notes.threshold_integrity: The rule >= 1.0 is held FIXED. It validated 3 members inside the registry's validated pro band and correctly rejected 4 ill-fitting gates. Per G3 the 4 non-validated branches stay inconclusive (3 decidable:false + 1 honest rule_not_met) rather than being relabeled by widening the rule.
notes.off_server_clarification: libpng (3949,3970,4020) and libxml2 (6910) corpora ARE on-disk on server B and WERE scored there (libpng_4020 validated at 14.664, the other three rule_not_met). So the decidable:false verdicts above are by TOOL REACH on the gate form, explicitly NOT by missing off-server corpus.
